message:
harness: migrate scheduler locking to parking_lot and introduce SchedulerLockExt trait

Refactor fsqlite-harness scheduler interactions to eliminate raw
std::sync::Mutex .lock().expect() boilerplate throughout the codebase.

Key changes:

- fslab.rs: Introduce SchedulerLockExt trait with schedule_task() method,
  providing a unified API across std::sync::MutexGuard, LockResult, and
  parking_lot::MutexGuard<LabScheduler>. This removes 9 separate
  .lock().expect("FsLab: scheduler lock poisoned").schedule() call sites
  and replaces them with the cleaner .lock(); .schedule_task() pattern.
  The parking_lot crate is now a regular dependency (moved from
  dev-dependencies in Cargo.toml) since SchedulerLockExt needs its
  MutexGuard type at the crate API level.

- fault_vfs.rs: Update the deterministic scheduling test to use the new
  SchedulerLockExt import and schedule_task() calls, aligning with the
  refactored FsLab API surface.

- supervision.rs: Fix bulkhead permit release test — replace explicit
  .release_to(&bulkhead) call with a simple permits.pop() drop, which
  is the correct RAII pattern for permit lifecycle. The old call was
  likely a leftover from an earlier API revision that no longer exists.

- validation_manifest.rs: Extract scenario ID constant
  VALIDATION_MANIFEST_SCENARIO_ID = "QUALITY-351" (previously hardcoded
  as "QUALITY-VALIDATION-MANIFEST" in multiple locations). Update the
  backend field in manifest log events from "fsqlite-harness" to
  "fsqlite" for consistency with the project naming convention. Update
  test fixtures to reference the new constant.

- validation_manifest_runner.rs: Import and use the new
  VALIDATION_MANIFEST_SCENARIO_ID constant instead of a local
  DEFAULT_SCENARIO_ID. Update help text to reflect the new default
  scenario ID (QUALITY-351).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+pub const VALIDATION_MANIFEST_SCENARIO_ID: &str = "QUALITY-351";
